mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates immediate capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO increases, or predictive IC drops below 0.01 across all rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index combined with a low federal funds rate creates negative real yields and forces expectations of aggressive future tightening, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0 and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains positive.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly supports corporate revenue expansion and wage-driven consumption, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with PAYEMS growth, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over 5-year rolling periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real gross domestic product confirms economic recession, triggering immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts, or the signal loses predictive power during recession onset periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and payroll employment signal synchronized supply and labor market strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 5-day returns in >50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.
